sections.paragraph:
fut. יִתְמֹךְ.-

(1) to take hold of, followed by an acc. Gen 48:17, and בְּ Pro 28:17 5:5 .

(2) to obtain, to acquire, e.g. honour, Pro 11:16 29:23 .

(3) to hold fast something taken, followed by an acc. Amo 1:5, . Metaph. Pro 4:4.

(4) to hold up, to support, followed by בְּ, Exo 17:12, “they held up his hands.” God is very often said to sustain any person or thing; followed by בְּ Psa 41:13 63:9 Isa 42:1 followed by an acc. Psa 16:5 17:5 .

(5) recipr. to take hold of each other, to hold together, to adhere; Job 36:17, דִּין וּמִשְׁפָּט יִתְמֹכוּ “cause and judgment follow one another;” compare אָחַז and לָקַד Hithp.

Niphal, pass. of No. 3; Pro 5:22.
